specificity.note: Names the exact figures (€2.45B base, 21.23% CAGR, ~55% unsourced PDU share, €495M ceiling, +€3.3M YAM at the €520 edge), the specific publisher (BIS/R&M), and the affected node (tamBase) with a concrete recentre value of 280.

nonObviousness.note: The ledger already caveats 'broader scope used as cross-check ceiling', but the risk supplies a genuinely new reason: the 21.23% growth signature is arithmetically incompatible with rack-PDU-only sources (5.4–9.8%), implying the ceiling measures the substitute architecture and the band is asymmetric. The whyMissable correctly notes the caveat itself camouflages the adversarial nature of the contamination.

mechanismDepth.note: Three-step chain with refs at each hop, and the trigger is arithmetic rather than hand-waved; but the propagation step's load-bearing claim — that excess growth sits in PSU/shelf revenue rather than report optimism — is admitted unverified in the likelihood rationale, so one arrow would take a hit from a hostile 'why?'.

evidenceQuality.note: Two attached BIS sources corroborate the CAGR/base arithmetic and the category's bundling of DC-power substitutes, and the rationale honestly flags the unverified 55% share and the OCP 12.4% counter-signal (one stance even marked 'contradicts'). Falls short of 4 because the key quantitative premise — segmentation showing material contamination — has no direct source.

decisionRelevance.note: The perturbation (tamBase set to 280) and the operational mitigation to size Year-1 inventory/hires against 240–320 directly change near-term spend, and both indicators (BIS segmentation pages, 2026 publisher refreshes) are concretely monitorable.
